conditions.B-treatment: code-analyze-mcp__analyze with parameter discovery (summary, cursor, page_size)

hypothesis.primary: Agents can discover and use new tool parameters (summary, cursor, page_size) to reduce token consumption without quality regression
hypothesis.mechanism: When parameter documentation is provided in the tool description, agents learn to apply summary=true for large outputs, use cursor for pagination, and tune page_size to limit response size
hypothesis.outcome_success: v7B uses new parameters in >40% of analyze calls, and token efficiency improves by >5% vs v6B with no quality loss

parameter_usage_tracking.summary: Detect summary=true in tool call inputs; count calls that use summary parameter
parameter_usage_tracking.cursor: Detect cursor presence and non-empty value in tool call inputs; counts pagination usage
parameter_usage_tracking.page_size: Detect page_size presence and non-null value in tool call inputs; counts size overrides
parameter_usage_tracking.instrumentation: validate.py and collect.py extract these from assistant message tool_use blocks

v6_baselines.note: v6 showed no quality regression (median A=10, median B=10). v7 tests whether parameter discovery reduces token overhead.

blinding.method: Random shuffle with seed=256, condition labels stripped before scoring

structural_accuracy.3: Correctly identifies all major modules, responsibilities, and data types; traces match codebase structure
structural_accuracy.2: Identifies most modules and responsibilities; minor omissions or generalization errors
structural_accuracy.1: Partial module identification; significant structural gaps or confusions
structural_accuracy.0: Misses major components; fundamentally incorrect structure

cross_module_tracing.3: Complete cross-module trace with intermediate types; call chains are accurate and detailed
cross_module_tracing.2: Traces key call chains; minor gaps or simplifications in type flow
cross_module_tracing.1: Partial trace; missing intermediate steps or types
cross_module_tracing.0: No meaningful trace or entirely incorrect

approach_quality.3: Well-reasoned change proposal; identifies all affected files, new types, and integration points; realistic risks
approach_quality.2: Reasonable proposal; most files and types identified; risks partially addressed
approach_quality.1: Incomplete or partially reasoned proposal; missing files or risk analysis
approach_quality.0: Superficial or incorrect proposal; major gaps

tool_efficiency.3: 5 or fewer analysis tool calls; focused exploration, clear path to synthesis
tool_efficiency.2: 6-10 analysis tool calls; somewhat exploratory but reaches conclusions
tool_efficiency.1: 11-20 analysis tool calls; extensive exploration; delayed synthesis
tool_efficiency.0: More than 20 analysis tool calls; inefficient, redundant, exploration-heavy

B5.note: extension registered as code-analyze (old name); summary not used; tool calls functionally equivalent

analysis_notes.primary_outcome: Parameter usage frequency and token reduction (v7B vs v6B)
analysis_notes.secondary_outcome: Quality maintenance (no regression in total scores)
analysis_notes.parameter_usage_goal: Target >40% of v7B runs using new parameters (summary, cursor, page_size)
cross_version_comparisons: v7B vs v6B: parameter optimization delta (token count reduction)
v7B vs v7A: gap closure (whether parameter discovery maintains quality advantage)
